name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Northern Arabian Sea — repositioned off eastern Oman coast, ~1,100km from Iran
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
notes: Day 21: CSG-3 remains primary strike platform for Operation Epic Fury. Continuously operating in Arabian Sea. CVW-9 with F-35Cs (VMFA-314) sustaining high-tempo operations — CENTCOM confirmed 7,800+ targets cumulative. Escort ships: USS Spruance (DDG-111), USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112). Up to 15+ Arleigh Burke destroyers total in region.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Defence Security Asia satellite analysis 3/15, CENTCOM 3/20

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: Naval Support Activity Souda Bay, Crete — pierside fire repairs
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
mission_summary: Pierside at Souda Bay, Crete for fire damage repairs — 266+ days deployed
notes: Day 21: Ford at Souda Bay, Crete for pierside fire repairs. Non-combat fire (Mar 12) destroyed 100+ beds, displaced 600+ sailors, required 30 hours to control. Nearly 200 treated for smoke inhalation. Ford deployed 266+ days (departed Norfolk June 24, 2025). Escorts remain on station — USS Bainbridge (DDG-96), USS Mahan (DDG-72), USS Winston S. Churchill (DDG-81) and others in Red Sea/Arabian Sea.
source: USNI News, Navy Times, Reuters, NYT 3/17, CNN 3/17, Kathimerini, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Atlantic / Eastern Mediterranean — preparing to deploy
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
mission_summary: Third carrier deploying — would create unprecedented three-carrier Middle East presence to backfill Ford
notes: Day 21: RADM Alexis Walker declared strike group 'ready to deploy today.' Multiple outlets report deployment imminent/underway toward Mediterranean to relieve Ford. No official deployment order publicly confirmed as of Mar 20. CVW-7 flew 1,586 sorties during 28-day COMPTUEX workup (Mar 5-6). CO Capt. Robert Bibeau.
source: USNI News 3/6, Navy Times 3/9, 19FortyFive 3/11, DPI 3/16, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Indian Ocean — exited Malacca Strait March 18, transiting to CENTCOM AOR
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 21: Exited Malacca Strait March 18 with USS San Diego (LPD-22) and USS New Orleans (LPD-18). ETA CENTCOM AOR: March 24-25. 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) with F-35B Lightning IIs (VMFA-121). Combined with USS Boxer ARG (departed San Diego Mar 19), this will bring ~4,400 additional Marines to the theater.
source: Reuters footage 3/17, USNI News 3/13, Axios 3/13, CNN, Nation Thailand, USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: CRITICAL: USS Tulsa/Santa Barbara in Singapore as of Mar 18 — USS Canberra location uncertain — MCM gap severe
vessels: LCS-30 USS Canberra (Indian Ocean — location uncertain)
LCS-16 USS Tulsa (Singapore as of Mar 18)
LCS-32 USS Santa Barbara (Singapore as of Mar 18)
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz — SEVERELY DEGRADED
notes: Day 21: CRITICAL MCM GAP. Christian Science Monitor published major exposé (Mar 20) on MCM capability deficit. USS Tulsa (LCS-16) and USS Santa Barbara (LCS-32) are in Singapore as of Mar 18 — thousands of miles from Bahrain. USS Canberra (LCS-30) somewhere in Indian Ocean. These are the only 3 dedicated US surface MCM platforms after 4 Avenger-class minesweepers decommissioned Jan 2026. Prior Navy assessment found 'unreliable unmanned vehicles, critical single-point failures, and sonar that couldn't see.' Gen. Caine downplayed mine threat — missiles, not mines, primary concern.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16, Washington Institute, Christian Science Monitor 3/20

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 19: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Conducted record 727-day deployment in 2022-2024. Weapons confirmed in use during conflict include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms >7,000 US targets struck, >15,000 combined by Day 14.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 19: Part of expanded ~15+ destroyer presence in region. Operating in Arabian Sea/Gulf of Oman supporting Lincoln's expanded destroyer screen. Eastern Med destroyers: USS Roosevelt (DDG-80), USS Bulkeley (DDG-84), USS Oscar Austin (DDG-79 — intercepted Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey using SM-3), USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116).
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Eastern Pacific — departed San Diego March 19, en route to Middle East
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid reinforcement — originally scheduled Indo-Pacific, rerouted and accelerated to Middle East
notes: Day 21: NEW DEPLOYMENT. Left San Diego March 19 with USS Comstock and USS Portland, carrying 2,200+ Marines from 11th MEU plus 2,000 sailors. Originally scheduled for Indo-Pacific deployment, rerouted and accelerated to Middle East. ETA approximately 3 weeks (early April). Combined with Tripoli ARG, will bring ~4,400 additional Marines to theater.
